recap:
- Unity Catalog (UC) acts as a centralized registry for managing assets in enterprise environments, providing a structured namespace for access control.
- Access control in UC allows for permission management on registered agents, similar to how permissions are managed on underlying data tables.
- UC tracks data lineage, documenting which data tables were used by agents for end-to-end traceability from raw documents to deployment.
- The governance workflow for agents includes defining a model signature, logging the model with an input example, and registering it with Unity Catalog.
- Agents must adhere to strict governance protocols to prevent unvalidated code pushes and ungoverned data access.
- The retrieval tool, if defined as a Unity Catalog Function, should be governed within the same catalog to ensure consistent security.
